title: Which Of The Following Are Indicators Of Trafficking In Persons
source_filename: Which Of The Following Are Indicators Of Trafficking In Persons.docx

qas.q: Which COCOM has a problem with trafficking in persons? (Select all that apply)
qas.a:
United States Southern Command
United States Indo-Pacific Command
United States Central Command
United States Africa Command
United States Northern Command
United States European Command

qas.q: Which of the following are indicators of trafficking in persons? (Select all that apply)
qas.a:
Injuries from violence or hazardous work conditions
Living or working in unsafe conditions
Observing someone being escorted or closely monitored at all times
Employees being required to work and live in the same location
Signs of physical abuse observed (i.e., bruises, cuts, burns, and broken bones)
Lack of identification (i.e., no passport or papers)

qas.q: The Trafficking Victims Protection Act does which of the following:
If you see signs and indicators of trafficking in persons what should you do:
qas.a: Report it immediately to your chain of command

qas.q: Children, including the children of military members, may be targets for traffickers online, at schools, or in neighborhoods.(post test
The Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000 does which of the following
In actual cases in the U.S. traffickers were (Select all that apply)
qas.a:
Service members, DoD civilians, and DoD contractors, DoD family members
Business owners
Members of organized crime or terrorist groups
Family members

qas.q: Trafficking in Persons only occurs in poor regions of the world.
One method for combating trafficking in persons is to be informed and learn the signs and indicators of trafficking in persons.
Large scale migration is a factor in the occurrence of trafficking in persons.
At which point should you report a trafficking in persons situation?
Which of the following signs most likely indicates a trafficking in persons situation?
qas.a: A person doesn't have control of their identification documents

qas.q: Suspect actions of traffickers include recruitment, harboring, transporting, providing, obtaining (and for sex trafficking patronizing or soliciting prostitution).
Which of the following is an appropriate action if you suspect a trafficking in persons violation? Select all that apply.
qas.a:
Contact the Department of Defense Inspector General (IG)
Contact your chain of command

qas.q: Which of the following is NOT a form of force, fraud or coercion used in human trafficking:
qas.a: Calling a person on the phone to talk about their life

qas.q: Traffickers have sophisticated business models involving new technologies including smartphones, social media, and specialized apps.

Prosecutable offenses under Article 134 of the UCMJ related to sex trafficking include prostitution, patronizing a prostitute, and pandering by compelling, inducing, enticing, or procuring an act of prostitution.
Victims of trafficking can be (Select all that apply):
qas.a:
Service members, DoD civilians, and DoD contractor employees, DoD family members
Foreign national or U.S. citizen
Adult or child
Female or male

qas.q: Any person under the age of 18 found in commercial sex is a per se victim and no proof of force, fraud, or coercion is needed.

You have a responsibility to report any trafficking in persons incidents you may witness, avoid establishments that show indicators of trafficking in persons, and report these establishments to your chain of command.
